question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger an unhandled runtime exception (e.g., NullPointerException or OutOfMemoryError) inside transactionExecutionService.execute() during a network partition, causing the finally block to skip restoreGasToBucket() and permanently drain the gas throttle bucket?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: restoreGasToBucket()] Can an unprivileged user craft a request with maximum gasLimit such that, during a network partition where transactionExecutionService.execute() throws an unchecked exception not caught by the catch blocks, result remains null and gasLimitToRestoreBaseline is never restored, causing the throttle bucket to be permanently depleted for all users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: callContract()] Can an unprivileged user submit a historical block query where recordFileService.findByBlockType() hangs indefinitely during a network partition, causing the blockSupplier Supplier to block all threads in the scoped context and produce a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: callContract()] Can an unprivileged user repeatedly trigger BlockNumberNotFoundException by querying non-existent historical blocks during a network partition, causing the memoized blockSupplier to cache the exception state and corrupt the ContractCallContext for subsequent legitimate requests sharing the same context?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the gap between the try block and the finally block in doProcessCall() during a network partition—where transactionExecutionService.execute() throws an Error (not Exception)—to bypass both catch clauses and skip restoreGasToBucket(), causing permanent gas bucket exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: restoreGasToBucket()] Can an unprivileged user send a request with gasLimit set to Long.MAX_VALUE during a network partition, causing integer overflow in the expression (gasLimit * throttleProperties.getGasLimitRefundPercent() / 100f) and producing a negative or zero gasLimitToRestoreBaseline, effectively never restoring gas to the throttle bucket?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a scenario during a network partition where MirrorEvmTransactionException is thrown with a null result field, causing restoreGasToBucket(null, gasLimit) to be called in the finally block and the null-check branch to restore only gasLimitToRestoreBaseline, potentially under-restoring gas and starving legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: callContract()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the ScopedValue-based ContractCallContext.run() during a network partition where the scoped context is never properly closed due to an Error thrown inside the lambda, causing context state to leak across requests and corrupt subsequent users' call parameters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged user send concurrent requests during a network partition such that multiple threads simultaneously enter the finally block of doProcessCall() and call throttleManager.restore() concurrently with the same gas values, causing a race condition that over-restores gas to the bucket and bypasses rate limiting?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: restoreGasToBucket()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the condition (!result.isSuccessful() && gasLimit == result.gasUsed()) during a network partition where the EVM returns a crafted result with gasUsed exactly equal to gasLimit, causing the service to always restore only gasLimitToRestoreBaseline instead of the actual remaining gas, enabling repeated high-gas calls that bypass throttling?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: callContract()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a network partition by flooding recordFileService.findByBlockType() with concurrent historical block queries, exhausting database connection pools and causing BlockNumberNotFoundException to be thrown for all users including legitimate ones?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged user send a request with estimate=true during a network partition to bypass the restoreGasToBucket() call in the finally block (guarded by !estimate), causing the gas throttle bucket to be permanently drained without any restoration?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/ContractCallService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doProcessCall()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the catch block for MirrorEvmTransactionException sets result = e.getResult() (which may be null) and then the finally block calls restoreGasToBucket(null, gasLimit), causing the null branch to restore only gasLimitToRestoreBaseline and under-restore gas during a network partition?
